name: Vzla Encuentra
db_description:
# Reencuentros

## Reunimos familias separadas por la emergencia humanitaria en Venezuela

**Reencuentros** es una plataforma web que usa reconocimiento facial para ayudar a localizar personas desaparecidas y reconectarlas con sus seres queridos.

---

## ¿Cómo funciona?

### Si estás buscando a alguien

Sube una foto de la persona que buscas. El sistema la compara con todos los registros de personas encontradas y te muestra los candidatos con mayor similitud facial, ordenados por nivel de coincidencia.

### Si encontraste a alguien

Registra a la persona con sus fotos y datos. Si alguien ya la está buscando, el sistema genera una alerta automática para conectar a ambas partes.

### Si quieres verificar antes de registrar

Subí una foto para saber si alguien está buscando a esa persona, sin necesidad de crear un registro.

---

## Características principales

- **Búsqueda por foto**: tecnología de reconocimiento facial que encuentra coincidencias incluso cuando no hay nombres ni documentos.
- **Protección de menores**: los nombres de niños, niñas y adolescentes se muestran protegidos en todas las respuestas públicas, incluso para administradores.
- **Alertas automáticas**: cuando un familiar busca a alguien que ya fue registrado como encontrado — o viceversa — el sistema avisa inmediatamente.
- **Moderación de contenido**: administradores revisan y aprueban las publicaciones antes de que aparezcan en búsquedas públicas.
- **Testimonios de reencuentros**: cualquier persona puede compartir fotos o videos de reuniones exitosas, inspirando a otras familias a no perder la esperanza.
- **Canales de reporte**: cualquier usuario puede reportar fallas técnicas o publicaciones inadecuadas.

---

## ¿Para quién es?

- **Familiares** que buscan a una persona desaparecida.
- **Rescatistas, voluntarios y organizaciones** que encuentran personas y necesitan identificar a quién avisar.
- **Administradores** que moderan contenido, gestionan reportes y mantienen la integridad de la plataforma.

---

## El problema que resolvemos

En contextos de crisis humanitaria, migración masiva y desapariciones, las familias quedan separadas sin canales efectivos para reencontrarse. Los métodos tradicionales (carteles, redes sociales, listas de nombres) dependen de que alguien reconozca un nombre o una foto.

Reencuentros usa reconocimiento facial para cerrar esa brecha: una foto vale más que mil nombres, especialmente cuando se trata de niños que no hablan, adultos en shock, o personas sin documentos.

---

## Privacidad y seguridad

- Los datos de menores se almacenan pero se protegen en todas las respuestas públicas.
- Solo administradores autenticados pueden moderar, eliminar o acceder al listado completo de registros.
- Las imágenes se almacenan de forma segura y las publicaciones pasan por moderación antes de ser visibles en búsquedas públicas.

---

Hecho con ❤️ para Venezuela.

analysis.summary: Reencuentros (leer_rostros) is a production-grade FastAPI facial-recognition service to reunite missing persons with families after disasters: familiares upload a photo to search, rescatistas register found persons, and pgvector/InsightFace ArcFace embeddings do similarity matching, with a real admin panel, moderation, minors-privacy masking, testimonios, reportes, traceability history, and a live CI/CD deploy pipeline to a real domain (vzlaencuentra.com / symtechven.com).
analysis.project_type: web app (FastAPI backend API + minimal static HTML admin/front) with facial recognition ML pipeline

frontend: single static frontend/index.html (305 lines) — thin admin/demo UI, not a full SPA
infra_deploy: GitHub Actions CD (deploy.yml, rsync+ssh to DigitalOcean droplet, pm2, nginx)

architecture.pattern: layered / bounded-context architecture: FastAPI routes -> use_cases -> repositories -> Postgres, with a pure domain layer (matching policy, privacy rules, entities) independent of SQL/HTTP
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: raw SQL via psycopg3 connection pool + pgvector extension, wrapped in per-context Repository classes (PersonaRepository, ReporteRepository, TestimonioRepository); no ORM
architecture.api_design: REST-ish JSON/multipart FastAPI endpoints, documented extensively via OpenAPI tags/descriptions, with legacy-array and paginated {data,meta} response variants for backward compatibility
architecture.separation_of_concerns: strong: domain/ (pure logic, no I/O), personas|reportes|testimonios/{repositories,use_cases}, shared/ (exceptions, helpers, SSRF-safe net), auth.py, storage.py, faces.py, database.py cleanly separated; main.py has no inline SQL for core tables
architecture.modularity: high — bounded-context folders each with their own repository + use_cases + fakes for testing
notable_patterns: Use-case pattern with dependency-injected repositories, testable via FakePersonaRepository/FakeReporteRepository without a DB or InsightFace
Domain exception -> HTTP status mapping centralized in _use_case_execute
Multi-embedding augmentation per photo (base + ±15° rotations) for pose robustness
MenoresPrivacy: minors' names masked below a confidence threshold across API boundary
SSRF-hardened URL image downloader (resolves + validates every redirect hop against private/loopback/link-local ranges) for the bulk-import endpoint
Idempotent schema migrations via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS + advisory lock to avoid multi-worker race on startup

code_organization.directory_structure: Clean bounded-context layout (app/{domain,personas,reportes,testimonios,shared}) mirrored in tests/, plus openspec/ change-management docs, scripts/ for local tooling and batch import, docker/ configs for standalone and compose deploys.
code_organization.naming_quality: Consistent Spanish domain naming (personas/buscada/encontrada) matching the business domain; use-case files named as verbs (RegistrarBusqueda, ModerarPersona) mapping 1:1 to endpoints.
code_organization.documentation_quality: Excellent — CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, ARQUITECTURA.md, API.md, DEPLOY.md, DOCKER.md, plus rich inline docstrings and an extensive OpenAPI description embedded in main.py.
code_organization.reasoning: Directory layout, naming and documentation all reflect a deliberately engineered, professionally organized codebase rather than hackathon scaffolding.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: .env-based config via pydantic-settings with .env.example and env_template.py; JWT_SECRET/DB creds not committed; CI uses GitHub Actions secrets (SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, SSH_HOST, SSH_USER) for deploy; app fails fast / warns loudly if JWT_SECRET is unset.
production_readiness.security_notes: bcrypt password hashing, JWT with expiry, admin-only endpoints via HTTPBearer dependency with is_active check, generic 401 to avoid user-enumeration, CORS restricted to real production origins (not '*'), SSRF protection on the URL-import endpoint (validates every redirect hop against private IP ranges, size-capped), minors' PII masked below match-confidence threshold. No rate limiting visible on public endpoints (login, search) which is a residual risk for brute force.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Uses a connection pool, pgvector HNSW index for fast ANN search at scale, pagination on all list endpoints, and multi-worker-safe schema init via Postgres advisory lock; InsightFace runs on CPUExecutionProvider only, which will be a throughput bottleneck under real load without GPU or horizontal scaling of the recognition step.
production_readiness.reasoning: Has real auth, structured error handling mapped to HTTP codes, environment-driven config, working CI (required status check) and CD (auto-deploy with health check) to an actual production domain — this is far beyond a hackathon demo in operational maturity; the main gaps are no structured logging/observability and no explicit rate limiting.

maturity.is_real_or_boilerplate: real, substantial hand/AI-engineered application with genuine domain logic, not boilerplate
maturity.reasoning: 12,259 LOC, 114 commits from 5 contributors, ~30 use-case test files with fake repositories achieving reported 100% coverage on use cases/domain/shared, working CI required-check + CD pipeline deploying to a live production host with a post-deploy health check, and iterative architecture-change tracking under openspec/.

viability.reasoning: This is already deployed to production (vzlaencuentra.com/symtechven.com) with a real facial-recognition matching pipeline, admin moderation, minors-privacy protections, and traceability history — directly addressing family reunification after a disaster; it is one of the most technically complete and operationally ready projects plausible for this hackathon category.

key_features: Facial similarity search (ArcFace/InsightFace + pgvector HNSW cosine) matching missing-person photos against found-person photos with confidence percentages
Dual-flow registration: familiar searches ('buscados') and rescatista registers found persons ('encontrados'), each surfacing reverse matches/alerts
Text-only fallback flow (no photo) matching by cédula/nombre for cases without a usable image
Traceability/history of sightings per found person (public trimmed view + full admin view with contact info)
Minors' PII masking below a match-confidence threshold across all public/admin responses
Admin panel: JWT+bcrypt auth, moderation of listings, reportes (bug/inappropriate content) triage, testimonios (reunion stories) approval workflow, real dashboard stats
Bulk import endpoint with SSRF-hardened image downloading and idempotency by external id
CI required test gate + automated CD to a live droplet with rsync, pm2 restart, and post-deploy health check
analysis.merge_potential: This is a strong, largely self-contained flagship candidate rather than a merge target — other simpler people-finder or shelter-mapping projects in the hackathon could integrate as data sources/importers (the /encontrados/importar bulk-import endpoint is explicitly designed for this) rather than this project merging into something else.
red_flags: No structured logging/observability (only print statements at startup) — will hamper incident response in a real deployment
No visible rate limiting on public endpoints like /admin/login or the search endpoints, leaving some brute-force/DoS exposure
InsightFace runs on CPUExecutionProvider only; will need infra scaling (GPU or horizontal workers) to handle real disaster-scale load
Frontend is a single 305-line static HTML file — the actual public-facing UI is minimal/thin compared to the backend's sophistication and would need real frontend work for broad public use

evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: Family separation after a disaster is one of the most acute needs in a humanitarian emergency — survivors, rescuers, and shelters have no fast way to cross-reference 'missing' reports against 'found' persons, especially for children, unconscious/shocked adults, or people without documents. VzlaEncuentra directly targets this with facial-recognition matching (ArcFace/InsightFace + pgvector similarity search) between 'buscados' and 'encontrados' registries, plus a text-fallback flow for cases without usable photos, minors' PII protection, and admin moderation to prevent abuse of a sensitive dataset.
evaluation.live_demo_notes: https://vzlaencuentra.com/ loads a real, installable PWA (manifest.webmanifest, Vite-built React SPA bundle at /assets/index-*.js returning 200 OK) titled "VzlaEncuentra — S.O.S. Búsqueda de personas" with copy explicitly referencing the Venezuela earthquake and facial recognition to reunite families. This sits on top of a genuinely production-grade FastAPI + PostgreSQL/pgvector backend (12,259 LOC, 114 commits, 5 contributors, CI required-check + automated CD to a DigitalOcean droplet with post-deploy health checks, JWT+bcrypt auth, SSRF-hardened bulk import, minors-privacy masking). Note: the technical insight report describes the frontend as a thin 305-line static admin HTML file, which understates what is actually deployed publicly — the live domain serves a full built SPA/PWA, not a bare static page.

diffusion.recommended_angle: Lead with the human story: a working facial-recognition tool, live today at vzlaencuentra.com, that can reunite Venezuelan families separated by the earthquake — upload one photo and get matched against everyone registered as found. Pair the live demo link with the Instagram reel to show it in action.
gaps: No confirmed real-user/real-match testimonial yet (a testimonios feature exists but needs a live success story for the post)
Could add a screenshot carousel of the actual search/upload flow for a static social post in addition to the video

evaluation.one_line_pitch: VzlaEncuentra: sube una foto y el reconocimiento facial reúne en minutos a las familias separadas por el terremoto — ya en vivo en vzlaencuentra.com.
